scenarios.title: Knowledge remains scoped to the character who learned it
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded tabletop RPG scene. Preserve character knowledge boundaries and player agency.

history.content: Mara privately tells Ilya that the obsidian key opens the west vault.
Ilya nods and hides the obsidian key inside her coat. Corvin remains outside and hears nothing.

scenarios.userMessage: Corvin joins us. I ask what he thinks the key opens.
referenceFacts: Only Mara and Ilya know the key opens the west vault.
Corvin must not know the vault fact without learning it.

scenarios.title: The active branch keeps the red-route decision
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded tabletop RPG scene. Follow only the active branch state.

history.content: At the fork, I reject the blue road and take the red road toward Ash Harbor.
You follow the red mile markers until Ash Harbor's lamps appear through the rain.

scenarios.userMessage: Where am I when dawn arrives, and which road did I take?

scenarios.title: Inventory and consequence remain aligned
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded tabletop RPG scene. Do not restore consumed or discarded items.

history.content: I spend my only sunstone to seal the breach.
The sunstone dissolves into gold dust as the breach seals. Your pack no longer contains it.

scenarios.userMessage: I search my pack for something that can light the catacombs.
referenceFacts: The narration must not put the sunstone back in inventory.
